heading: Reports
label: 11273 Reports
notes: CodificationSection was formerly classified to , The Public Health and Welfare, prior to editorial reclassification and renumbering as this section. Some section numbers or references in amendment notes below reflect the classification of such sections or references prior to editorial reclassification.section 5715 of Title 42
Prior ProvisionsA prior was renumbered section 383 and is classified to .section 382 of Pub. L. 93–415section 11274 of this title

Amendments1999— amended section generally, making reporting requirements biennial rather than annual and adding subsec. headings.Pub. L. 106–711992—, which directed the amendment of section “361 of the Juvenile Justice and Delinquency Act of 1974 ()” by amending it generally and adding subsec. (b), was executed to this section, which is section 381 of the Juvenile Justice and Delinquency Prevention Act of 1974 (), to reflect the probable intent of Congress and the intervening renumbering of as section 381 by . Prior to amendment, this section consisted of subsecs. (a) and (b) which required annual reports to Congress on the status and accomplishments of the runaway and homeless youth centers funded under part A of this subchapter and of the transitional living youth projects funded under part B of this subchapter.Pub. L. 102–586, § 3(h)42 U.S.C. 5715Pub. L. 93–415section 361 of Pub. L. 93–415section 3(g)(1)(A)(ii) of Pub. L. 102–5861989—Subsec. (a). , substituted “submit a report to the Committee on Education and Labor of the House of Representatives and the Committee on the Judiciary of the Senate” for “report to the Congress”.Pub. L. 101–204, § 1003(1)Subsec. (b). , substituted “Not later than 180 days after the end of each fiscal year, the Secretary shall submit a report to the Committee on Education and Labor of the House of Representatives and the Committee on the Judiciary of the Senate” for “The Secretary shall annually report to the Congress”.Pub. L. 101–204, § 1003(2)1988—Subsec. (a). , §§ 7271(c)(5), 7273(c)(1), (2), 7274, designated existing provisions as subsec. (a), in introductory provisions substituted “Not later than 180 days after the end of each fiscal year, the Secretary shall” for “The Secretary shall annually”, “runaway and homeless youth centers” for “runaway centers”, and “part A of this subchapter” for “this part”, and in par. (1) substituted “runaway and homeless youth” for “runaway youth”.Pub. L. 100–690Subsec. (b). , added subsec. (b).Pub. L. 100–690, § 7273(c)(3)1980— substituted “centers” for “houses”.Pub. L. 96–509

Effective Date of 1988 AmendmentAmendment by effective , with the report required by this section with respect to fiscal year 1988 to be submitted not later than , notwithstanding the 180-day period provided in this section, see section 7296(a), (b)(3) of , set out as a note under .Pub. children.label: A
children.text: alleviating the problems of runaway and homeless youth;

children.label: B
children.text: if applicable or appropriate, reuniting such youth with their families and encouraging the resolution of intrafamily problems through counseling and other services;

children.label: C
children.text: strengthening family relationships and encouraging stable living conditions for such youth; and

children.label: D
children.text: assisting such youth to decide upon a future course of action; and

children.label: A
children.text: the number and characteristics of homeless youth served by such projects;

children.label: B
children.text: the types of activities carried out by such projects;

children.label: C
children.text: the effectiveness of such projects in alleviating the problems of homeless youth;

children.label: D
children.text: the effectiveness of such projects in preparing homeless youth for self-sufficiency;

children.label: E
children.text: the effectiveness of such projects in assisting homeless youth to decide upon future education, employment, and independent living;

children.label: F
children.text: the ability of such projects to encourage the resolution of intrafamily problems through counseling and development of self-sufficient living skills; and

children.label: G
children.text: activities and programs planned by such projects for the following fiscal year.
the evaluations performed by the Secretary under ; andsection 11277 of this title
descriptions of the qualifications of, and training provided to, individuals involved in carrying out such evaluations.
